Accelerock.notes: Lycanroc-Dusk's priority Rock; blocked by Armor Tail/Psychic Terrain.

Ally Switch.notes: Swaps the user's position with its ally in priority — dodges targeted nukes (Farigiraf filler slot).

Aqua Jet.notes: Priority finisher for Basculegion/Azumarill; halved in sun; blocked by Armor Tail/Psychic Terrain.

Aura Sphere.notes: Never misses; x4 on Kingambit and Tyranitar; Fighting does 0 vs Ghost (Annihilape, Sinistcha).

Aurora Veil.notes: Only usable in snow; halves ALL damage to your side for 5 turns (Froslass-Mega/Ninetales-Alola engine); does NOT stop status, Perish Song or Encore.

Baton Pass.notes: Passes stat boosts to the switch-in (Espathra Speed Boost archetype); beaten by Taunt and phazing (Whirlwind).

Blizzard.notes: 100% accuracy in snow (Froslass-Mega STAB); x4 on Garchomp.

Body Press.notes: Damages off the user's Defense — Stamina Archaludon's Body Press snowballs and hits Kingambit/Corviknight x4.

Brave Bird.notes: 33% recoil — Talonflame chips itself out fast; priority at full HP via Gale Wings; Oranguru Instruct enables double Brave Bird.

Bullet Punch.notes: Priority Steel (Lucario, Metagross-Mega) — physical answer to Sylveon/Floette; blocked by Armor Tail/Psychic Terrain.

Calm Mind.notes: +1 SpA/SpD — boosted Floette-Mega bounces special hits; answer with physical attacks only (it does not raise Defense), double-target before it stacks, Perish timer ignores boosts.

Clanging Scales.notes: 110 BP spread that drops the user's Def; it is a sound move — silenced by Throat Chop, blocked by Soundproof.

Clangorous Soul.notes: +1 to ALL stats at the cost of 33% max HP (Kommo-o); it is a sound move — Throat Chop silences it; punish the setup turn while it sits at -33% HP.

Close Combat.notes: Drops the user's Def/SpD — Sneasler autocombo: White Herb restores the drops, the item is consumed, Unburden doubles Speed; does 0 vs Ghost; x4 on Kingambit/Tyranitar.

Crunch.notes: Normal-bracket Dark answer to Farigiraf (Armor Tail blocks Sucker Punch, not Crunch); 20% Def drop.

Dazzling Gleam.notes: Spread Fairy (Floette-Mega, Hatterene); ignores redirection like all spread moves.

Dire Claw.notes: 50% chance of poison, paralysis or sleep (Sneasler); physical answer to Sylveon/Floette.

Disable.notes: Seals the target's last move — Mega Gengar pairs it with Shadow Tag to lock trapped answers; switching cures it.

Double-Edge.notes: 33% recoil; with Aerilate (Salamence-Mega) it becomes a boosted Flying attack — the source describes it as his spread option (single-target in mainline; flagged).

Draco Meteor.notes: -2 SpA self after use (Dragonite-Mega nuke); Fairy is immune.

Dragon Claw.notes: Garchomp's safe Dragon STAB; Fairy is immune.

Dragon Darts.notes: Hits twice; in doubles it sends one dart at each opponent (Dragapult); Fairy immunity (Tinkaton) blanks it entirely.

Dragon Pulse.notes: Archaludon's secondary STAB; boosted by Mega Launcher; Fairy is immune.

Drain Punch.notes: Heals the user for 50% of damage dealt (Annihilape staple); does 0 vs Ghost.

Dual Wingbeat.notes: Hits twice (Aerodactyl-Mega) — double hits break Focus Sash and Multiscale.

Earth Power.notes: 10% SpD drop; x4 on Glimmora from special Ground attackers.

Earthquake.notes: Hits EVERYONE else including your ally — pair with Flying/Levitate partners or have them Protect; Pelipper/Dragonite and other Flying are immune; blocked by Wide Guard.

Electro Shot.notes: Grants +1 SpA on use; fires instantly in rain — WITHOUT rain it spends a turn charging (sun teams blank it). Archaludon's rain cannon: KO him within 2 turns or he snowballs.

Encore.notes: Locks the target into its last move for 3 turns; applied before the target acts it CANCELS its chosen move that turn (faster Encorer wins); switching cures it; a fresh switch-in cannot be Encored. Premier answer to Protect/Shell Smash/Trick Room/Fake Out.

Eruption.notes: 150 BP at full HP, scales down as the user takes damage; Torkoal fires it in sun under Trick Room.

Expanding Force.notes: In Psychic Terrain (Indeedee): boosted power AND hits both opponents; Dark-types (Kingambit) are immune.

Extreme Speed.notes: +2 priority outspeeds other priority (Dragonite-Mega filler); does 0 vs Ghost; blocked by Armor Tail/Psychic Terrain.

Facade.notes: Doubles in power when the user is statused — Guts Ursaluna turns burn/poison into a buff; Normal: does 0 vs Ghost.

Fake Out.notes: Guaranteed flinch but ONLY works on the user's first turn out; Normal — does 0 vs Ghost (Basculegion, Sinistcha, Gengar); blocked by Armor Tail and Psychic Terrain; Encore seals repeat attempts.

Flamethrower.notes: 10% burn; boosted 50% in sun, halved in rain; never into Thermal Exchange Baxcalibur.

Flare Blitz.notes: 33% recoil, 10% burn; Incineroar's only real damage output.

Flash Cannon.notes: 10% SpD drop (Archaludon's Steel STAB option over Body Press).

Flip Turn.notes: Pivot: damages then switches out — escapes Shadow Tag trapping and resets Perish counters (Basculegion/Palafin).

Flower Trick.notes: Never misses and ALWAYS crits (Meowscarada) — ignores screens and defensive boosts via the guaranteed crit.

Follow Me.notes: Redirects single-target moves to the user for one turn (Maushold, Indeedee, Clefable); does NOT redirect spread or sound moves; ends if the user leaves/faints mid-turn; bypassed by Stalwart/Propeller Tail; Quash neuters the turn.

Freeze-Dry.notes: Ice move that is super effective on Water (x2) — Ninetales-Alola's anti-rain tech.

Giga Drain.notes: Drains 50% of damage dealt; THE Grass answer to Rotom-Wash and Dondozo; Venusaur's sun STAB.

Gigaton Hammer.notes: Huge but CANNOT be used twice in a row (Tinkaton) — alternate with Fake Out/Encore/Protect; one of the few physical nukes that drops boosted Floette-Mega.

Glaive Rush.notes: After use the user takes double damage and cannot be missed until its next turn (Baxcalibur).

Grassy Glide.notes: +1 priority ONLY in Grassy Terrain (Rillaboom + Grassy Surge); priority Grass that punishes Basculegion/Politoed; blocked by Armor Tail/Psychic Terrain when boosted.

Headlong Rush.notes: Drops the user's Def/SpD after use (Ursaluna Trick Room nuke).

Heat Wave.notes: Spread Fire (Charizard-Y/Delphox-M staple); +50% in sun, -50% in rain; blocked by Wide Guard; never into Thermal Exchange Baxcalibur (+1 Atk gift).

Helping Hand.notes: +50% power to the ally's move this turn — Politoed Helping Hand + Palafin Jet Punch OHKOs even neutral targets.

High Jump Kick.notes: User crashes for 50% max HP on miss/Protect; Scrappy Lopunny-Mega lands it on Ghosts.

Hurricane.notes: 100% accuracy in rain (Pelipper, Dragonite-Mega, Vivillon); 30% confusion; the Flying answer to Scovillain/Amoonguss.

Hydro Pump.notes: +50% in rain, halved in sun (Blastoise-Mega, Rotom-Wash).

Hyper Voice.notes: Spread + sound: ignores Follow Me/Rage Powder entirely; Pixilate Sylveon turns it into a Fairy spread nuke; blocked by Soundproof, silenced by Throat Chop; as Normal it does 0 vs Ghost (until Pixilate converts it).

Icicle Spear.notes: Hits 2-5 times (Baxcalibur) — multi-hits break Focus Sash and Multiscale.

Icy Wind.notes: Spread -1 Spe speed control (Politoed option); x4 on Garchomp.

Instruct.notes: The partner immediately repeats its last move = two attacks per turn (Oranguru; e.g. double Brave Bird) — KO the director to stop it.

Iron Head.notes: 30% flinch; x4 on Ninetales-Alola, the physical Steel answer to Sylveon/Floette.

Jet Punch.notes: Palafin's priority Water; Helping Hand-boosted it OHKOs even neutral targets; blocked by Armor Tail/Psychic Terrain; halved in sun.

King's Shield.notes: Protects AND returns Aegislash to tanky Shield form; contact attackers get their Attack dropped.

Knock Off.notes: Removes the target's item (boosted damage if it holds one) — Meowscarada utility; NOT in Incineroar's Champions M-A movepool (removed).

Kowtow Cleave.notes: Never misses (Kingambit STAB); normal-bracket Dark that Armor Tail does NOT block (unlike Sucker Punch).

Last Respects.notes: 50 + 50 per fainted ally (150 at 2 down) — Adaptability Basculegion turns it into a wipe; never feed free KOs, keep Basculegion for last; double-KO plans (Perish) charge it; does 0 vs Normal-types.

Leech Seed.notes: Drains the target each turn (Scovillain-Mega pairs it with Rage Powder); does not affect Grass-types; switching sheds the seed.

Life Dew.notes: Heals the user AND its ally (Sinistcha's support slot).

Light of Ruin.notes: Floette-Mega's signature nuke; 50% recoil of damage dealt.

Light Screen.notes: Halves special damage to your side for 5 turns (Prankster Sableye/Grimmsnarl/Klefki); screens do NOT stop status, Perish Song or Encore.

Liquidation.notes: Basculegion's no-recoil Water STAB option (vs Wave Crash); 20% Def drop; halved in sun.

Low Kick.notes: Weight-based, up to 120 vs heavy targets (Kingambit, Tyranitar, Dondozo); x4 on Kingambit; does 0 vs Ghost.

Lumina Crash.notes: Drops the target's SpD by 2 on every hit (Espathra) — snowballs fast; Dark-types are immune.

Matcha Gotcha.notes: Sinistcha's spread STAB: drains 50% of damage dealt (the 'heals' in its kit) and can burn.

Moonblast.notes: 30% SpA drop; THE Fairy button: x4 on Kommo-o/Hydreigon, x2 on Garchomp/Dragonite, and Sableye's only weakness.

Mystical Fire.notes: Drops the target's SpA on hit (Sylveon coverage vs Steels).

Overheat.notes: -2 SpA self after use; the Italian source's 'Vampata' on Scovillain-Mega = Overheat (not Incinerate).

Parting Shot.notes: Lowers the target's Atk/SpA then pivots out — BYPASSES Shadow Tag trapping and resets Perish counters; the Incineroar Intimidate-cycle engine; sound move (Soundproof blocks it) and it feeds Defiant/Competitive.

Perish Song.notes: Counter 3 on ALL Pokemon present including the singer; -1 each end of turn, KO at 0; switching RESETS the counter (trapped mons can't); Soundproof and Good as Gold are immune, Magic Bounce REFLECTS it, Throat Chop silences the singer; re-singing does not refresh existing counters; ignores boosts, Sash, screens, Unaware, Magic Guard; the double KO it scores charges enemy Last Respects/Supreme Overlord.

Phantom Force.notes: Two-turn move (vanishes, then strikes through Protect) — Dragapult option; does 0 vs Normal-types.

Play Rough.notes: Physical Fairy (Azumarill, Mawile-Mega); x4 deletes Kommo-o.

Pollen Puff.notes: Damages enemies, but aimed at the ally it HEALS instead (Amoonguss support tech).

Poltergeist.notes: FAILS if the target holds no item (Aegislash STAB); does 0 vs Normal-types.

Population Bomb.notes: Hits up to 10 times (Maushold); Normal — does 0 vs Ghost; multi-hits shred Focus Sash/Multiscale.

Power Gem.notes: Glimmora's special Rock STAB — special Rock answer to Charizard-Y/Talonflame.

Protect.notes: Default fourth slot of the format; scouts Sucker Punch (which fails vs Protect), stalls weather/Tailwind/Trick Room turns; Encore punishes predictable Protects.

Psychic.notes: The designated Sneasler killer (x2 and it dies); Dark-types (Kingambit, Incineroar) are fully immune; boosted in Psychic Terrain.

Quash.notes: Forces the target to act LAST this turn — Sableye's soft anti-redirect/anti-threat tech (Quash the redirector, then nuke past it).

Quick Attack.notes: Priority finisher (Sylveon filler); does 0 vs Ghost; blocked by Armor Tail/Psychic Terrain.

Rage Fist.notes: +50 BP for every hit the user has TAKEN — never chip Annihilape, KO it fast; does 0 vs Normal-types.

Rage Powder.notes: Redirects single-target moves for one turn (Sinistcha, Amoonguss, Scovillain, Vivillon); it is POWDER — Grass-types and Overcoat ignore it; spread/sound moves go through anyway; bypassed by Stalwart/Propeller Tail; dies if the redirector leaves mid-turn.

Rain Dance.notes: Manual rain, 5 turns (Sableye tech to re-win the weather war — the LAST weather set wins).

Reflect.notes: Halves physical damage to your side for 5 turns (Prankster Sableye/Grimmsnarl/Klefki); screens do NOT stop status, Perish Song or Encore.

Rock Slide.notes: Spread with 30% flinch; the designated Charizard-Y killer — it is everywhere (Garchomp, Tyranitar, Aerodactyl, Kleavor, Lycanroc); blocked by Wide Guard.

Sacred Sword.notes: Ignores the target's stat changes; x4 on Kingambit/Tyranitar (Aegislash); does 0 vs Ghost.

Shadow Ball.notes: 20% SpD drop; does 0 vs Normal-types (Farigiraf, Indeedee, Snorlax, Maushold, Lopunny, Kangaskhan, Ursaluna) — Ghost attackers struggle into them.

Shadow Sneak.notes: Priority Ghost (Aegislash) — the listed answer to Basculegion before Last Respects snowballs; does 0 vs Normal; blocked by Armor Tail/Psychic Terrain.

Shell Smash.notes: +2 Atk/SpA/Spe, -1 Def/SpD (Blastoise-Mega) — Encore it or KO before the setup pays off.

Slack Off.notes: Restores 50% of the user's max HP (Hippowdon recovery).

Sleep Powder.notes: Powder sleep — Grass-types and Overcoat are immune; Compound Eyes Vivillon pushes it to 97%; Safety Goggles do NOT exist in Champions, Lum Berry is the only out.

Sludge Bomb.notes: 30% poison; special Poison answer to Fairies (Sylveon/Floette/Whimsicott); Steel is immune.

Snarl.notes: Spread sound that drops both targets' SpA (Incineroar variant); ignores redirection; blocked by Soundproof.

Solar Beam.notes: Instant in sun — Charizard-Y's surprise on Waters; WITHOUT sun it spends a turn charging, and in rain it is also halved: never click it off-weather.

Spiky Shield.notes: Protect variant that chips contact attackers (Glimmora).

Spirit Break.notes: Drops the target's SpA on every hit (Grimmsnarl); part of the Fairy package that deletes Kommo-o (x4).

Spore.notes: 100% sleep but POWDER: Grass-types and Overcoat are immune (Amoonguss); reflected by Magic Bounce; Safety Goggles do NOT exist in Champions — Lum Berry is the only item answer.

Stomping Tantrum.notes: Doubles in power if the user's previous move failed (Garchomp's single-target Ground that spares the ally, unlike Earthquake).

Stone Axe.notes: Sets Stealth Rock splinters on hit (Kleavor's Scarf STAB) — chips every enemy switch-in afterward.

Sucker Punch.notes: Priority Dark but FAILS if the target is not using an attacking move (Protect/status/switch) — the Kingambit mind-game; blocked by Armor Tail and Psychic Terrain.

Tailwind.notes: Doubles your side's Speed for 4 turns — the format's main speed control; Prankster Whimsicott sets it with priority, Aerodactyl-Mega is the fastest setter, Talonflame gets it priority via Gale Wings; Taunt the setters to stop it.

Taunt.notes: Blocks only STATUS moves — a Taunted mon can still attack; shuts down Trick Room/Tailwind/Perish setters; Prankster makes it priority (but Armor Tail blocks priority Taunt; the slow non-priority Taunt still lands on Farigiraf); Magic Bounce reflects it; Good as Gold is immune; switching cures it.

Teleport.notes: Negative-priority pivot — even Shadow Tag-trapped mons escape the Perish cage with it and reset their counter.

Throat Chop.notes: The target cannot use SOUND moves for 2 turns (Perish Song, Hyper Voice, Clanging Scales, Clangorous Soul) — Incineroar's anti-sound variant; switching clears the silence; Throat Chop itself is NOT a sound move; dual-singer Perish teams need two Chops.

Thunder.notes: 100% accuracy in rain; 30% paralysis; Ground is immune.

Thunder Wave.notes: Paralysis (Prankster Klefki) — ruins Unburden Sneasler and every speed-based plan; Ground is immune.

Thunderbolt.notes: The Pelipper deleter (x4) and the safe answer to Water/Flying (Farigiraf, Rotom-Wash); Ground is immune.

Trick Room.notes: 5 turns; inverts move order WITHIN each priority bracket — priority moves still go first; setters of note: Hatterene (Magic Bounce reflects Taunt), Indeedee/Farigiraf (can't be Fake Outed), Oranguru, Sinistcha; counter with Taunt before setup, Encore on the TR, or stall the 5 turns.

U-turn.notes: Pivot: damages then switches — escapes Shadow Tag and resets Perish counters; NOT in Incineroar's Champions M-A movepool (removed); flagged uncertain on Dragapult ('U-turn?').

Volt Switch.notes: Pivot: damages then switches — escapes Shadow Tag and resets Perish counters (some Rotom-Wash builds); Ground immunity stops both damage and pivot.

Water Spout.notes: 150 BP spread at full HP, scales down with damage taken (Blastoise-Mega) — pressure it early; halved in sun, boosted in rain.

Wave Crash.notes: 33% recoil; Basculegion's (and Palafin's) heavy Water STAB — monstrous in rain with Adaptability, halved in sun.

Weather Ball.notes: Becomes the active weather's type with doubled power (Fire in sun for Charizard-Y, Water in rain for Pelipper/Politoed) — a free second STAB for weather engines; plain 50 BP Normal without weather.

Whirlwind.notes: Phazes the target out into a random teammate (Skarmory-Mega) — breaks Baton Pass chains and setup; negative priority.

Wide Guard.notes: Blocks SPREAD moves for the turn (Heat Wave, Rock Slide, Earthquake, Hyper Voice...) but not single-target ones (Aerodactyl-Mega, Pelipper); appears as 'Bodyguard' in the Italian UI.

Will-O-Wisp.notes: Burn halves physical damage (Prankster Sableye, Rotom-Wash); Fire-types and Thermal Exchange Baxcalibur cannot be burned; reflected by Magic Bounce.

Yawn.notes: Target falls asleep at the end of NEXT turn unless it switches — forces a switch or a sleep (Sylveon filler); reflected by Magic Bounce.

Bug Buzz.notes: Special Bug STAB; sound move, so Soundproof blocks it and Throat Chop disables it.

Energy Ball.notes: Special Grass STAB; 10% SpD drop; hits Water/Ground/Rock targets.

Leech Life.notes: Physical Bug STAB that heals the user for half the damage dealt.

Power Whip.notes: High-power physical Grass STAB; punishes Water, Ground, and Rock targets but can miss.

Acrobatics.notes: Doubles to 110 BP when the user has no item; common no-item Talonflame coverage in Champions data.

Coaching.notes: Boosts the ally's Attack and Defense by one stage.

Darkest Lariat.notes: Physical Dark attack that ignores the target's Defense/evasion stat changes.

Draining Kiss.notes: Special Fairy attack that heals the user for 75% of the damage dealt.

Electroweb.notes: Spread Electric speed-control move; lowers targets' Speed by one stage.

Feint.notes: Priority chip that can hit through Protect-style protection and remove the protection for the turn.

Foul Play.notes: Uses the target's Attack stat for damage; useful on passive support Pokemon.

Hyper Beam.notes: High-power single-target Normal attack; the user must recharge after it. Pixilate turns it Fairy for Sylveon.

Ice Beam.notes: Reliable special Ice coverage; 10% freeze chance.

Ice Fang.notes: Physical Ice coverage; 10% freeze chance and 10% flinch chance.

Imprison.notes: Prevents opponents from using moves that the user also knows; common anti-Trick Room tech.

Quick Guard.notes: Protects the user's side from priority moves for the turn.

Rock Tomb.notes: Single-target Rock coverage that lowers the target's Speed by one stage.

Scale Shot.notes: Hits 2-5 times, then raises the user's Speed and lowers its Defense.

Sunny Day.notes: Sets sun for 5 turns; enables Solar Beam, Chlorophyll, and Fire damage boosts.

Super Fang.notes: Cuts the target's current HP in half; does no damage to Ghost types.

Twin Beam.notes: Hits twice; Farigiraf signature Psychic attack.

Water Pulse.notes: Mega Launcher-boosted pulse move on Mega Blastoise; 20% confusion chance.

Bug Bite.notes: Technician-boosted physical Bug STAB for Scizor; eats the target's held Berry in mainline mechanics.

Bulk Up.notes: Raises Attack and Defense by one stage; common Corviknight setup option in Champions data.

Roost.notes: Heals 50% HP; removes Flying typing for the rest of the turn in mainline mechanics.

Triple Axel.notes: Hits up to three times with increasing power; Tsareena uses it for Dragon/Flying/Grass coverage.

Trop Kick.notes: Physical Grass STAB that lowers the target's Attack by one stage.
